mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses Australian history including British colonization, self-government, federation, the commonwealth, and the White Australia policy (B).
British colonial settlement and governance in Australia, New Zealand, and across Oceania are central to outline A-C.
British colonization of Australia and New Zealand, colonial government, and establishment of the Australian commonwealth connect to outline B-C.
The effects of colonization on indigenous peoples of Oceania and colonial-settler dynamics inform A.3, B.4, and C.2.
European exploration of Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia -- including Cook's voyages -- is foundational to A.3 and B.1.
The federation movement in Australia (1860-1901) and the development of national identity are central to B.2.
The White Australia immigration policy and patterns of British colonial migration shaped the demographics discussed in B.2-B.3.
Australia's cooperation with Britain in World War I is relevant to outline B.3.
The prehistory of Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia provides deep context for A.2.
Ethnography of the diverse peoples of Oceania, Aboriginal populations, and Maori people illuminates A.2, B.4, and C.2.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationaleAI: The geography of Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia provides a foundation for understanding regional development (A.2).
